signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.11 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite the failed US-Iran peace deal and subsequent Brent oil surge to $99.9 (+4.65%). JPY COT LF net short -61,340 (crowded carry) remains vulnerable to a volatility spike, but VIX at 17.2 (normal volatility) suggests carry remains the dominant regime. Intervention risk rises on a move toward 160.00 as Middle East tensions introduce non-linear JPY safe-haven demand.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (long% at 18.5% extreme) vs RBNZ market bias for a hike at the 27 May meeting. Specs are aggressively fading a hawkish central bank while NZD/USD trades at 0.5944. If RBNZ delivers a hike or hawkish guidance, a crowded short-unwind risk is elevated. Watch for a break above 0.6000 to confirm spec capitulation.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7232 — COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) vs retail 89% short. Institutional and retail positioning are at opposing extremes, creating a high-conviction divergence. Retail is fading the 2.74% 1M rally while institutions remain heavily bid on the 60bp AUD carry premium over USD. Retail capitulation risk is high if price extends toward 0.7300.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + ECB Stance
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.709 vs 252d norm of -0.135 (z=+1.87σ). The pair is moving in lockstep with European equities rather than its historical inverse relationship, suggesting EUR is being traded as a pro-cyclical risk asset. ECB's de Guindos downplaying energy shock risks supports the 2% hold stance. EUR/USD at 1.1755 remains capped by the 175bp USD carry advantage.
